title: Imitated From The Japanese
content:
A most astonishing thing --
Seventy years have I lived;

(Hurrah for the flowers of Spring,
For Spring is here again.)

Seventy years have I lived
No ragged beggar-man,
Seventy years have I lived,
Seventy years man and boy,
And never have I danced for joy.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Butler Yeats delivers a powerful performance in "Imitated From The Japanese"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
